questions.stem: <ruby>日本<rt>にほん</rt></ruby>に<ruby>来<rt>き</rt></ruby>＿＿＿のころは、<ruby>何<rt>なに</rt></ruby>も<ruby>分<rt>わ</rt></ruby>からなかった。
questions.why: たばかり = only a short span has passed since the action ('back when I had just arrived'). たとたん marks the instant of the action and cannot take のころ.

questions.stem: この<ruby>時計<rt>とけい</rt></ruby>は<ruby>買<rt>か</rt></ruby>っ＿＿＿なのに、もう<ruby>壊<rt>こわ</rt></ruby>れてしまった。
questions.why: ばかり expresses the SPEAKER'S SENSE that little time has passed, which is exactly what なのに pushes against. たところ is the objective 'just now' moment and sits awkwardly before なのに.

questions.stem: <ruby>彼<rt>かれ</rt></ruby>は<ruby>大学<rt>だいがく</rt></ruby>を<ruby>卒業<rt>そつぎょう</rt></ruby>し＿＿＿で、まだ<ruby>働<rt>はたら</rt></ruby>いたことがない。
questions.why: He graduated only recently. たとたん would need an immediate consequence, not a description of his state.
